title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: 1. High: URL-only quote posts now fail at runtime. `extract_quote_tweet_id()` can return `("", tweet_id)` for posts that are just an X URL, but [`post_tweet`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py:69) still rejects empty text, so [`scheduler.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:509) will raise instead of posting the quote. 2. Medium: Quote URL detection is still inconsistent and misses normal `twitter.com/<user>/status/...` links because the substring check uses `twitter.com/status/` in [`scheduler.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:504) and [`scheduler.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:268). The new regex supports those URLs, but the gating logic does not. 3. Medium: `_QUOTE_URL_RE` is too greedy: `\S*` removes any trailing non-space characters after the URL, so punctuation or adjacent Japanese text can be silently dropped from the authored post in [`x_poster.py`](#/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py:14). 4. No direct security issues in this diff; main risk is posting regressions from the new quote-tweet path.
